preview_text:
The model favors Spain at 75%, a commanding margin over Belgium's 4% win probability. This asymmetry reflects a substantial gap in the forecast. Belgium enters as a heavy underdog under the statistical framework.

The W/D/L spread widens the gap further. The model assigns a draw only 20% likelihood, suggesting the match is unlikely to remain level. Spain's three-in-four baseline conceals a heavily skewed distribution; Belgium's path to three points narrows sharply. The fixture is modeled as competitive in execution but decisive in outcome—Spain expected to control possession and chances.

The model and sportsbook consensus diverge markedly. Bookmakers assign Spain 58%, some 17 percentage points lower than the model, while pricing Belgium's upset odds at 17% versus the model's 4%. Polymarket aligns closely with the sportsbook consensus. The dashboard does not adjudicate which view of Belgium's chances is correct; the gap is material enough that bettors should note the discrepancy before placing stakes.
